case_title: PORTFOLIO RECOVERY ASSOCIATES, LLC VS. FATAI ADEWUSI et al

docket_entries.description: REQUEST FOR ENTRY OF DEFAULT/DEFAULT ENTERED, DECLARATION OF NON-MILITARY STATUS AS TO DEFENDANT ADEWUSI, FATAI
MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-26-2015 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND IS CONTINUED FROM JAN-26-2015 TO FEB-10-2015, AT 9:30 AM, IN DEPT. 302, TO GIVE OPPOSING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. OPPOSING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN JANUARY 26, 2015 AT 4:00 P.M. OR THE MOTION WILL BE GRANTED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING AND REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON, II, CLERK: SEAN KANE, NOT REPORTED; DEPT. 302 (302/AJR)

DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFFS OPPOSITION TO RELIEF FROM DEFAULT (TRANSACTION ID # 15006019) FILED BY PLAINTIFF PORTFOLIO RECOVERY ASSOCIATES, LLC

LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHALL FILE ITS PROPOSED ANSWER WITHIN 10 DAYS OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. THE DECLARATION OF FATAI ADEWUSI ESTABLISHES THAT DEFENDANT DID NOT KNOW ABOUT THE CASE UNTIL AFTER HIS DEFAULT WAS ENTERED AND, THEREFORE, DEFENDANT IS ENTITLED TO RELIEF PURSUANT TO CCP SEC. 473.5. DEFENDANT DID NOT UNREASONABLY DELAY IN BRINGING THIS MOTION, AS HIS DECLARATION ESTABLISHES THAT HE ATTEMPTED TO SETTLE WITH PLAINTIFF BEFORE FILING THIS MOTION. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE DAWN GHENO, CSR #7489; DEPT 302. (302/EHG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-09-2015 Set For Trial On Jul-27-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Mar-25-2015 Off Calendar

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Feb-10-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT FATAI ADEWUSI'S MOTION FOR ORDER SETTING ASIDE DEFAULT AND GRANTING DEFENDANT LEAVE TO FILE ANSWER is granted. Defendant shall file its proposed answer within 10 days of entry of this order. "[T]he law strongly favors trial and disposition on the merits." (Elston v. Turlock (1985) 38 Cal.3d 227, 233.) The Declaration of Fatai Adewusi establishes that Defendant did not know about the case until after his default was entered and, therefore, Defendant is entitled to relief pursuant to CCP sec. 473.5. Defendant did not unreasonably delay in bringing this motion, as his declaration establishes that he attempted to settle with Plaintiff before filing this motion. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Jan-26-2015 Continued To Feb-10-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT FATAI ADEWUSI'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is continued to February 10, 2015 to give opposing party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Opposing party must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than January 26, 2015 at 4:00 p.m. or the motion will be granted. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers. Judge: A. James Robertson, II, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/AJR)
